prompt: An employer procures a £40m office via JCT Design and Build 2016. Who carries primary design risk after novation of concept design?

options.text: Contractor for completion of design to meet employer's requirements, subject to ERs and liability caps
Employer retains all design risk without exception
Architect remains solely liable after novation always
RICS APC assessor carries design risk

explanation: JCT D&B allocates design completion risk to the contractor within ER framework.

distractorExplanations.b: D&B transfers design completion risk to contractor per contract.
distractorExplanations.c: Novation reallocates design responsibility per deed terms.
distractorExplanations.d: Contract parties allocate design risk, not assessors.

prompt: Under NEC4 ECC Option C, target cost mechanics mean:

options.text: Fixed lump sum with no open-book accounting
Defined cost plus fee with pain/gain share against agreed target at completion

explanation: NEC Option C combines target cost with defined cost and pain/gain share.

distractorExplanations.a: Option C is target contract with defined cost openness.
distractorExplanations.c: Option C uses defined cost, not dayworks as primary mechanism.
distractorExplanations.d: Option C expressly includes pain/gain arrangements.

prompt: FIDIC Red Book 2017 on an overseas infrastructure job — interim payments are based on:

options.text: UK JCT interim certificate rules automatically
Measured works per BOQ and contract valuation procedures in Conditions of Contract
Final account only with no interim payments

explanation: FIDIC Red Book interim payments follow contract BOQ and valuation clauses.

distractorExplanations.a: FIDIC payments follow contractual measurement and certification.
distractorExplanations.b: Applicable contract is FIDIC, not JCT.

prompt: A two-stage tender is chosen for a complex hospital. Stage 1 typically awards:

options.text: Design responsibility to employer only with no contractor input
Pre-construction services agreement with defined prelim design, programme, and stage 2 competition scope

explanation: Two-stage procurement separates pre-construction engagement from stage 2 price fix.

distractorExplanations.a: Stage 1 sets up PCSA/pre-construction with stage 2 price competition.
distractorExplanations.b: Two-stage engages contractor early for buildability and pricing.

prompt: JCT Standard Building Contract with quantities — variations are valued using:

options.text: Contract valuation rules referencing BOQ rates and fair rates hierarchy
RICS Red Book only, superseding JCT
No valuation until 10 years after completion

explanation: JCT SBC variations use contractual rate hierarchy for valuation.

distractorExplanations.c: Contract conditions govern unless parties agree otherwise.
distractorExplanations.d: Variations are valued during the contract.

prompt: NEC early warning meetings primarily aim to:

options.text: Notify and mitigate risk events collaboratively before they become compensation events

explanation: NEC early warnings are collaborative risk mitigation, core to contract culture.

distractorExplanations.a: Early warnings support CE management, not replace it.
distractorExplanations.d: Programme remains central to NEC compensation events.

prompt: Procurement on a public sector framework — call-off requires:

options.text: Verbal award to lowest informal quote
Compliance with PCR2015/Procurement Act procedures and framework terms for mini-competition or direct award

explanation: Public sector framework call-offs must comply with procurement regulations.

distractorExplanations.a: Public procurement must follow statutory framework rules.
distractorExplanations.b: Framework call-offs need documented compliant process.

prompt: JCT Intermediate Building Contract is most appropriate when:

options.text: Contractor designs entire building with no ERs
Employer design with contractor carrying works risk; moderate complexity without full SBC administration

explanation: JCT IBC suits employer-designed projects of moderate complexity.

distractorExplanations.a: That scenario typically uses Design & Build.
distractorExplanations.b: IBC still requires defined employer design information.
distractorExplanations.c: Minor works use JCT MW, not Intermediate.

prompt: A tenderer submits lowest price but fails insurance requirements. QS recommendation?

options.text: Non-compliant — reject or seek cure per tender rules; do not recommend award on price alone
Award anyway and fix insurance later

explanation: Tender evaluation applies pass/fail compliance before price ranking.

distractorExplanations.c: Non-compliant tenders are excluded per evaluation criteria.

prompt: NEC compensation event for adverse weather values by:

options.text: Contractor diary entry only without data
Forecast vs actual weather data per contract data with defined compensation thresholds
JCT extension of time rules in NEC contract

explanation: NEC weather compensation events use contract weather data and thresholds.

distractorExplanations.a: NEC CEs require contractual evidence and procedure.
distractorExplanations.c: Weather CEs are not blanket percentages.
distractorExplanations.d: NEC has its own compensation event mechanism.

prompt: Design-and-build ERs are silent on acoustic performance. Tender risk sits with:

options.text: No party — acoustic performance not required
Contractor if ERs plus applicable law imply performance; clarify via tender query urgently

explanation: Silent ERs in D&B create contractor pricing risk requiring pre-tender clarification.

distractorExplanations.a: Silence creates dispute — D&B contractors price implied compliance.
distractorExplanations.b: Statutory and functional requirements may still apply.
distractorExplanations.d: Contract parties must clarify scope operationally.

prompt: Single-stage selective tender with 4 invited contractors — advantage is:

options.text: Competitive price with controlled bidder quality and reduced open-market unknowns

explanation: Selective tender balances competition with bidder quality control.

distractorExplanations.a: Selective tender limits bidders to pre-qualified firms.
distractorExplanations.b: Selective tender still requires full tender pack.
distractorExplanations.c: Variations can arise on any contract form.

prompt: JCT clause 2.29 liquidated damages must be:

options.text: Genuine pre-estimate of loss, not penalty, and clearly stated in contract particulars
Any punitive sum to deter delay

explanation: JCT liquidated damages require genuine pre-estimate and clear contract statement.

distractorExplanations.b: UK law restricts penalties; LDs must be genuine pre-estimate.
distractorExplanations.d: LDs are typically stated rates/amounts per contract.

prompt: FIDIC Yellow Book (design-build) employer provides:

options.text: Detailed BOQ only with no performance requirements
Employer's requirements; contractor designs and executes works to meet them

explanation: FIDIC Yellow Book follows design-build on employer's requirements.

distractorExplanations.a: Yellow Book is design-build on employer's requirements.
distractorExplanations.d: Yellow Book is design-build, not Red Book remeasurement.

prompt: Pre-qualification questionnaire purpose in procurement is:

options.text: Assess financial standing, experience, H&S, and capacity before inviting tender

explanation: PQQ ensures only capable contractors proceed to tender.

distractorExplanations.a: PQQ filters bidders; tender follows for shortlisted firms.
distractorExplanations.d: Insurance is typically verified at PQQ/tender stage.

prompt: NEC4 Option E (cost reimbursable) suits:

options.text: Fully defined BOQ school extension only
When contractor should carry all price risk fixed
High uncertainty scopes needing open-book defined cost with pain/gain or fee control

explanation: NEC Option E fits uncertain scope with open-book cost reimbursement.

distractorExplanations.a: Defined BOQ projects often suit Option A/B better.
distractorExplanations.b: Option E requires defined cost openness.
distractorExplanations.c: Option E is cost reimbursable, not fixed price.

prompt: A contractor notices conflicting specs between drawings and ERs at tender. Best action?

options.text: Submit compliant tender query requesting priority and clarification before submission

explanation: Pre-tender queries resolve document conflicts for fair pricing.

distractorExplanations.b: Priority of documents is contract-specific and must be clarified.
distractorExplanations.d: Query process exists to resolve conflicts.

prompt: JCT payment notice and pay less notice regime requires:

options.text: Specified notices within statutory/contractual deadlines showing sums due and deductions

explanation: JCT payment regime requires timely payment and pay less notices.

distractorExplanations.a: Housing Grants Act and JCT require prescribed notices.
distractorExplanations.c: Payment cycles and notices are time-bound.
distractorExplanations.d: Pay less notices must follow prescribed procedure.

prompt: Negotiated tender without competition is justified when:

options.text: Unique specialist supplier, continuity risk, or procurement rules allow — with documented rationale

explanation: Negotiated tender requires documented justification and compliance with rules.

distractorExplanations.b: Negotiated routes still require commercial documentation.
distractorExplanations.d: Competition should be default unless exceptions apply.

prompt: NEC Project Manager issues instruction changing scope. Contractor should:

options.text: Comply and notify compensation event if not own fault within NEC timescales

explanation: NEC instructions trigger compensation event procedures when applicable.

distractorExplanations.a: NEC requires compliance subject to CE notification.
distractorExplanations.c: Instructions are part of normal NEC administration.

prompt: Retention under JCT typically is:

options.text: Percentage withheld from interim payments released at practical completion and defects period end

explanation: JCT retention provides performance security with staged release.

distractorExplanations.b: Retention is security, largely released per contract.
distractorExplanations.c: Retention is withheld from contractor payments.
distractorExplanations.d: Retention is common subject to contract and policy.

prompt: OJEU/Procurement Act threshold exceeded — next step is:

options.text: Structured advertised procedure with defined evaluation criteria and standstill
Informal emails to three firms only

explanation: Above-threshold public procurement requires statutory advertised procedures.

prompt: JCT Minor Works contract limitations include:

options.text: Includes full design liability on employer always
Simpler administration for low-risk small works — not for complex major projects

explanation: JCT MW suits simple small works with reduced administration.

distractorExplanations.a: Minor Works is for simpler low-value works.
distractorExplanations.b: MW can include contractor design options but is limited in scope.
distractorExplanations.d: Contract selection depends on project risk and parties.

prompt: Contractor's tender qualification 'subject to ground conditions' should be:

options.text: Ignored because all tenders are equal
Evaluated — may be non-compliant if tender required unconditional acceptance of site info

explanation: Tender qualifications affect compliance and risk — require employer assessment.

distractorExplanations.a: Qualifications affect compliance and risk allocation.
distractorExplanations.b: Employer must assess qualification impact on risk and price.
distractorExplanations.c: Qualifications may disqualify or require clarification.

prompt: FIDIC dispute avoidance/adjudication board role is:

options.text: Early dispute avoidance and binding adjudication decisions per FIDIC procedure

explanation: FIDIC DAAB provides contractual dispute avoidance and adjudication.

distractorExplanations.b: DAAB supports dispute resolution, not employer functions.
distractorExplanations.d: DAAB is dispute avoidance/adjudication, not design.
